title: Eschweilera ovata
taxonomy: Eschweilera ovata is classified within the family Lecythidaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Brazil nut family. This family consists of approximately 30 genera and 270 species, many of which are found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Eschweilera ovata is further classified as a member of the genus Eschweilera, which consists of around 130 species found primarily in the tropical forests of Central and South America. The genus Eschweilera is characterized by its evergreen trees and shrubs, which often produce showy white or yellow flowers. Overall, the classification of Eschweilera ovata reflects its unique characteristics and its place within the diverse family of Lecythidaceae.
uses: Eschweilera ovata has been found to possess various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine practices in its native regions. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and to exhibit antioxidant properties. preparations: Eschweilera ovata is known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in herbal preparations. intro: Eschweilera ovata is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae, commonly known as the white guava. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eschweilera ovata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
